authority.summary: Authority repo for topology, shared vocabulary, exported mapping snapshots, and failover doctrine.

A.label: Audio survives independently
A.minimumOutcome: Main audio path remains playable even if controller or visual runtimes are absent.
requiredCapabilities: Mixer to PA path remains intact
Emergency blackout or safe visual path is documented

B.label: One controller path and one visual path survive
B.minimumOutcome: At least one controller route and one controllable visual endpoint remain available.
requiredCapabilities: Direct MIDI or OSC can bypass the preferred UI
At least one visual endpoint or utility visual path remains available

C.label: Optional enrichment
C.minimumOutcome: Analysis, secondary endpoints, and lab outputs can drop out without ending the set.
requiredCapabilities: Analysis lane can disappear without blocking scene recall
Offline or lab repos are never the only source of a required control path

macro.purpose: Human-operated continuous control lane for scene shaping and live overrides.

analysis.purpose: Normalized audio-reactive bias lane that enriches but never gates the visual system.

semantic.purpose: Discrete scene, state, and event trigger lane used by UI, bridge, and hardware fallback controls.

invariants: Endpoints follow clock; they never originate global transport.
Canonical scene, state, and event IDs are owned by the shared rig contract, not by endpoint repos.
MSVP and nw_wrld or wrld_on operate as peer endpoints behind the same contract.
degradation: If analysis is unavailable, endpoints continue from scene base plus manual macro input only.
If one endpoint is unavailable, switch to the other endpoint or a documented clean-camera or blackout utility path.
If the preferred UI is unavailable, direct MIDI or OSC still targets the same semantic IDs through the bridge.
If the authority repo is unavailable at runtime, exported snapshots remain sufficient for consumers.

scenes.label: Intro
scenes.description: Establishing scene family shared across controller and endpoint layers.

scenes.label: Soft
scenes.description: Low-intensity scene family for calmer or more spacious visuals.

scenes.label: Crash
scenes.description: High-intensity scene family for harsh or saturated moments.

scenes.label: Clean Camera
scenes.description: Minimal-processing visual state that preserves documentary clarity.

states.label: Blackout
states.description: Fastest safe visual kill and reset state.

states.label: Manual Override
states.description: State where direct human control takes priority over automation or weighted modulation.

events.label: Noise Burst
events.description: Bounded hit event for hybrid audiovisual impact.

events.label: Glitch Hit
events.description: Short-lived event that spikes a glitch treatment without changing the long-form scene.

macros.label: Mixer Morph
macros.description: Crossfades between current visual layers or worlds.

macros.label: Frame Buffer Feedback
macros.description: Controls visual memory or echo intensity.

macros.label: Mosh
macros.description: Controls aggressive glitch or stream-collision intensity.

macros.label: ReTrace Density
macros.description: Controls line or contour density in trace-based worlds.

macros.label: Texture Detail
macros.description: Controls grit or fine-texture detail in texture-oriented worlds.

macros.label: Analysis Blend
macros.description: Crossfades between analysis influence and manual macro authority.

analysis.label: Low Band
analysis.description: Bass-weighted energy from the analysis lane.

analysis.label: Mid Band
analysis.description: Midrange body energy from the analysis lane.

analysis.label: Upper Mid Band
analysis.description: Presence or abrasive energy from the analysis lane.

analysis.label: High Band
analysis.description: High-frequency air, cymbal, or hiss energy from the analysis lane.

bindings.label: Blackout
bindings.notes: Global blackout binding that should remain reachable under failure.

repoRegistry.summary: Owns topology, vocabulary, failover doctrine, and exported snapshots. It is not a runtime dependency on show night.
Preferred performer UI that emits the shared semantic lane plus concrete MIDI and OSC bindings.
produces: semantic scene, state, and event triggers

fallbacks.target: Edirol plus Maschine direct into bridge
fallbacks.outcome: Shared scene and state IDs remain reachable without the preferred UI.

repoRegistry.summary: Publishes normalized analysis outputs and band mappings only. It never owns scenes, transport, or endpoint-specific logic.
Beat-synced visual endpoint that consumes shared scenes, states, macros, and analysis without redefining the contract.

fallbacks.target: nw_wrld or clean-camera or blackout utility path
fallbacks.outcome: One visual endpoint survives without requiring MSVP.

repoRegistry.summary: Runtime visual platform for project-driven visuals. It remains a peer endpoint rather than the contract authority.

fallbacks.target: MSVP or clean-camera or SCapps utility path
fallbacks.outcome: One visual endpoint survives without requiring nw_wrld.

repoRegistry.summary: Project content repo layered on top of nw_wrld. It carries workspace modules and assets, not canonical scene naming.
consumes: shared semantic IDs for workspace wiring

fallbacks.outcome: The set retains a visual path without requiring this workspace.

repoRegistry.summary: R and D repo for offline assets, optional live sources, and experimentation. It never becomes the only source of a required control path.
consumes: shared control-bus naming when promoted into live use

notes: Unsupported commands should fail visibly and safely.
Health states are simple enough to mirror in UI, bridge logs, or endpoint consoles.
